subject: islamic-studies
description: Kalimas & Doors of Jannat MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: What is the name of the 1st Kalima?
mcqs.explanation: The 1st Kalima is Kalima Tayyaba, meaning 'The Pure Word.' It is La ilaha illallah Muhammadur Rasulullah.

mcqs.q: What does 'Tayyaba' mean in Kalima Tayyaba?
mcqs.explanation: 'Tayyaba' means 'Pure' or 'Good.' Kalima Tayyaba = The Pure Word.

mcqs.q: How many words does Kalima Tayyaba contain in Arabic?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba (La ilaha illallah Muhammadur Rasulullah) consists of 7 words in Arabic.

mcqs.q: How many letters does Kalima Tayyaba contain in Arabic?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba contains 48 letters in Arabic. This is a commonly tested fact in Pakistani competitive exams.

mcqs.q: What is the technical term for reciting Kalima Tayyaba?
mcqs.explanation: The recitation of Kalima Tayyaba (La ilaha illallah) is called Tahleel (تهليل). Tasbih = SubhanAllah; Takbeer = Allahu Akbar; Tahmeed = Alhamdulillah.

mcqs.q: The first part of Kalima Tayyaba (La ilaha illallah) appears in which Surah?
mcqs.explanation: La ilaha illallah appears in Surah Al-Imran, verse 18 (3:18). The second part (Muhammadur Rasulullah) is in Surah Al-Fath (48:29).

mcqs.q: The second part of Kalima Tayyaba (Muhammadur Rasulullah) appears in which Surah?
mcqs.explanation: Muhammadur Rasulullah appears in Surah Al-Fath, verse 29 (48:29). The first part (La ilaha illallah) is in Surah Al-Imran (3:18).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is the correct translation of Kalima Tayyaba?
options: There is no god but Allah; Muhammad (PBUH) is the Messenger of Allah
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba = La ilaha illallah Muhammadur Rasulullah = There is no god but Allah; Muhammad (PBUH) is the Messenger of Allah.

mcqs.q: What is the name of the 2nd Kalima?
mcqs.explanation: The 2nd Kalima is Kalima Shahadat — the Word of Testimony.

mcqs.q: What does Kalima Shahadat mean?
mcqs.explanation: Shahadat means testimony/witness. Kalima Shahadat is the Word of Testimony — a declaration of faith in Allah and His Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: What is the 3rd Kalima called?
mcqs.explanation: The 3rd Kalima is Kalima Tamjeed — the Word of Glorification (glorifying and praising Allah).

mcqs.q: 'Tamjeed' means which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Tamjeed (تمجيد) means glorification — praising Allah's greatness and majesty.

mcqs.q: What is the 4th Kalima called?
mcqs.explanation: The 4th Kalima is Kalima Tauheed — the Word of Oneness, affirming Allah's absolute oneness.

mcqs.q: Which Kalima is called 'The Word of Seeking Forgiveness'?
mcqs.explanation: The 5th Kalima — Kalima Astaghfar — is the Word of Seeking Forgiveness. Astaghfar means seeking pardon from Allah.

mcqs.q: What is the 6th and last Kalima called?
mcqs.explanation: The 6th Kalima is Kalima Radde Kufr — the Word of Rejecting Disbelief. It rejects all forms of Kufr and Shirk.

mcqs.q: 'Radde Kufr' means which of the following?
mcqs.explanation: Radde Kufr means Rejection of Disbelief (Kufr). This Kalima involves rejecting all forms of disbelief and Shirk.

mcqs.q: How many Kalimas are there in South Asian Islamic education?
mcqs.explanation: There are 6 Kalimas widely taught in South Asian Islamic education: Tayyaba, Shahadat, Tamjeed, Tauheed, Astaghfar, and Radde Kufr.

mcqs.q: Which Kalima is recited by a person when they first embrace Islam?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba (La ilaha illallah Muhammadur Rasulullah) is the first declaration recited when a person enters Islam.

mcqs.q: How many gates (doors) does Jannah have?
mcqs.explanation: Jannah has 8 gates. This is established by authentic Hadith in Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim.

mcqs.q: How many gates does Jahannam (Hell) have?
mcqs.explanation: Jahannam has 7 gates, as mentioned in Surah Al-Hijr (15:44): 'It has seven gates; for each gate is of them a portion designated.'

mcqs.q: The Hadith about the eight gates of Jannah is found in which books?
mcqs.explanation: The Hadith about the eight gates of Jannah is narrated in both Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim.

mcqs.q: What is the name of the 1st door of Jannah?
mcqs.explanation: The 1st door of Jannah is Baab As-Salaat, reserved for those who were punctual in their prayers (Salaat).

mcqs.q: Through which door of Jannah will those who died in defence of Islam enter?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Jihad (the Door of Jihad) is reserved for those who strived and died in the defence of Islam.

mcqs.q: What is the name of the 3rd door of Jannah?
mcqs.explanation: The 3rd door of Jannah is Baab As-Sadaqah, reserved for those who frequently gave to charity (Sadaqah).

mcqs.q: What is the name of the special door of Jannah reserved for those who fasted?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Ar-Rayyaan is the door of Jannah exclusively for those who observed fasting, especially in Ramadan.

mcqs.q: What does 'Ar-Rayyaan' mean?
mcqs.explanation: Rayyaan (ريان) means 'well-watered' or 'lush' — a fitting reward for those who endured hunger and thirst during fasts.

mcqs.q: Which door of Jannah is for those who performed Hajj?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Hajj — the Door of Pilgrimage — is reserved for those who performed the Hajj.

mcqs.q: Which is the 6th door of Jannah?
options: Baab Al-Kaazimeen Al-Ghaiz Wal Aafina Anin Naas
mcqs.explanation: The 6th door is Baab Al-Kaazimeen Al-Ghaiz Wal Aafina Anin Naas — for those who suppress anger and forgive everyone.

mcqs.q: The 6th door of Jannah is reserved for those who:
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Kaazimeen Al-Ghaiz Wal Aafina Anin Naas is for those who control their anger and forgive others — reflecting the Islamic emphasis on forbearance (hilm).

mcqs.q: What is the 7th door of Jannah called?
mcqs.explanation: The 7th door is Baab Al-Iman — reserved for those who have sincere faith and trust in Allah.

mcqs.q: Baab Al-Dhikr is the ______ door of Jannah.
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Dhikr is the 8th and last door of Jannah, reserved for those who constantly remember Allah (Dhikr).

mcqs.q: Through which door of Jannah will those who constantly remembered Allah enter?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Dhikr (the Door of Remembrance) is reserved for those who constantly and excessively engaged in Dhikr — remembrance of Allah.

mcqs.q: Which door of Jannah is most frequently tested in PPSC/FPSC papers?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Ar-Rayyaan (the fasting gate) is the most commonly tested door of Jannah in competitive exams because of its distinctive name and specific eligibility.

mcqs.q: Which is the longest-named door of Jannah?
options: Baab Al-Kaazimeen Al-Ghaiz Wal Aafina Anin Naas
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Kaazimeen Al-Ghaiz Wal Aafina Anin Naas is the longest-named gate of Jannah.

mcqs.q: According to Hadith, a person who excels in all acts of worship can enter Jannah through:
options: The gate of the Prophet's choice
mcqs.explanation: According to a Hadith in Sahih Muslim, a person who performs all forms of worship can enter Jannah through any of the eight gates.

mcqs.q: The Hadith about the eight gates of Jannah is narrated by which Companion?
mcqs.explanation: The famous Hadith about the eight gates of Jannah is narrated by Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) in Sahih Muslim.

mcqs.q: Which Kalima directly relates to the concept of Tawheed (Oneness of Allah)?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tauheed (the 4th Kalima) directly affirms the concept of Tawheed — the absolute and undivided oneness of Allah.

mcqs.q: The word 'Kalima' in Arabic means:
mcqs.explanation: 'Kalima' (كلمة) in Arabic means 'word' or 'statement.' The six Kalimas are six important statements of Islamic faith.

mcqs.q: Kalima Astaghfar is a prayer for:
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Astaghfar (the 5th Kalima) is the Word of Seeking Forgiveness — a prayer for Allah's pardon and purification of the soul.

mcqs.q: Which Kalima combines declaration of faith in Allah AND His Prophet (PBUH)?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba = La ilaha illallah (faith in Allah) + Muhammadur Rasulullah (faith in the Prophet PBUH). It is the foundational dual declaration.

mcqs.q: Surah Al-Hijr verse 44 mentions:
mcqs.explanation: Surah Al-Hijr (15:44) states: 'It has seven gates; for each gate is of them a portion designated.' — referring to the 7 gates of Jahannam.

mcqs.q: Kalima Tamjeed specifically involves:
mcqs.explanation: Tamjeed means glorification. Kalima Tamjeed (3rd Kalima) involves praising and glorifying Allah through phrases like SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, etc.

mcqs.q: Which pillar of Islam is directly reflected in Baab Ar-Rayyaan?
mcqs.explanation: Baab Ar-Rayyaan is the door for those who fasted — directly corresponding to Sawm (Fasting), the 3rd Pillar of Islam.

mcqs.q: The 5th door Baab Al-Hajj corresponds to which Pillar of Islam?
mcqs.explanation: The 5th door is Baab Al-Hajj — corresponding directly to the 5th Pillar of Islam, Hajj (Pilgrimage).

mcqs.q: Which of the following is NOT one of the six Kalimas?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Noor is not one of the six Kalimas. The six are: Tayyaba, Shahadat, Tamjeed, Tauheed, Astaghfar, and Radde Kufr.

mcqs.q: 'Kufr' in the name Kalima Radde Kufr means:
mcqs.explanation: Kufr (كفر) means disbelief or rejection of Islamic faith. Radde Kufr means Rejection of Disbelief.

mcqs.q: Baab Al-Jihad is for Muslims who:
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Jihad is specifically reserved for those who strived and gave their lives in the defence of Islam.

mcqs.q: Which number Kalima is Kalima Astaghfar?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Astaghfar is the 5th Kalima in the sequence of six Kalimas.

mcqs.q: Kalima Tayyaba forms the basis of which Pillar of Islam?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tayyaba (La ilaha illallah Muhammadur Rasulullah) forms the basis of Shahadah — the 1st Pillar of Islam.

mcqs.q: Through Baab Al-Iman, who will enter Jannah?
options: Only those who prayed 5 times daily
Those with sincere faith who followed Allah's commands
mcqs.explanation: Baab Al-Iman (7th door) is for those who have sincere faith (Iman) in Allah and fulfilled their life according to His commands.

mcqs.q: Baab As-Sadaqah admits those who gave charity to all of the following EXCEPT:
mcqs.explanation: Baab As-Sadaqah is for those who gave to parents, orphans, the helpless, and travellers in need. Giving to wealthy merchants is not Sadaqah in this context.

mcqs.q: Which Kalima is recited during Hajj as the pilgrims' key declaration?
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Tauheed is the Kalima specifically recited during Hajj — affirming the absolute oneness of Allah during the pilgrimage.

mcqs.q: Kalima Shahadat is the formal declaration recited when:
mcqs.explanation: Kalima Shahadat is the formal declaration of faith recited when a person accepts or converts to Islam. It bears witness to Allah and His Prophet (PBUH).

mcqs.q: How many marks do Kalimas and Doors of Jannah MCQs typically carry in PPSC One Paper Islamiat?
mcqs.explanation: Kalimas and Doors of Jannah topics typically yield 2–5 MCQs in PPSC and FPSC One Paper exams. Most frequently tested: Kalima Tayyaba details, name of fasting gate (Baab Ar-Rayyaan), total gates of Jannah and Jahannam.
